##comment|Paste this whole document into the input area and hit 'build page' to see all the components as of 1 April 2026. This first line is a comment and will not be visible on-screen (but will appear as a comment in the code).
##goback
##alert_ho|This page is an example|It has been created using the scratchpad tool, written in 'scratchdown'|success
##spacer|10
##h1|Welcome to this auto-generated page|Example content
##h2|What's this all about then? (this is a h2)
##p|This layout has been generated using the scratchdown language at the top of the page. 

This component is part of a paragraph. It can contain multiple lines, like this.

Another component type is inset:

##inset|Components are reusable parts of a user interface. Using pre-built, core elements allows government teams to build consistent services.

You can also use the individual components in different patterns and contexts. For example, you can use the Text input component to ask for an email address, a National Insurance number or someone’s name.

Each component in the GOV.UK Design System has guidance on its use and coded examples. If you’re using the GOV.UK Prototype Kit, or have included GOV.UK Frontend in your build, coded examples will render the same as in the Design System...
##p|Nice! You can also include standalone links:
##a|The gov.uk web site|https://www.gov.uk
##hr
##Row
##Column|100
##h2|1. Input components
##input|Your name
##input|Any previous surnames you have had
##input|Any nicknames (Like 'Diggle' or 'Frampton')
##a|Add another|mylink/mypage
##spacer|20|
##textarea|Your address (Please include any relevant floor number)
##select|Country of birth|Choose...|Angiers|Belgium|Canada|Denmark|Ecuador|France
##date|Your date of birth(For example 23 09 1942)
##character_count|Other notes (This is the 'character count' component)|6|250
##End Column
##End Row
##h3|And now... something in three columns
##Row
##Column|25
##checkboxes|Support needed|Housing|Medical|Other
##End Column
##Column|25
##radios|Choose size|Small|Medium|Large
##End Column
##Column|50
##radios|Choose cost (This reveals more)|Cheap:chp1|Medium:med1|Pricey:pr1
##hidden_p|What's cheap?|chp1
##hidden_checkbox|1 - 10|chp1
##hidden_checkbox|11-20|chp1
##hidden_checkbox|21-30|chp1
##hidden_textarea|Give us your definition of 'medium'|med1
##hidden_select|How honest are you being?||Very|Moderately|Er...|med1
##hidden_date|When did you start needing the finer things in life?|pr1
##End Column
##End Row
##p|We used an hr earlier in the page, you can also used 'spacers' and even colour them in:
##spacer|5|#ddd
##h2|2. Output components
##p|Using column widths of 40% and 75% to ensure they are easy to scan. 
##row
##column|75
##tasklist|Tasklist: your progress|This is the name of the first task (With a hint):/qu1/:Completed|Second task:/qu2/: Incomplete|Task the third:/qu3/:Incomplete|The final and fourth task:/qu4/: Cannot be started
##end column
##end row
##Row
##Column|40
##summarylist|Summary list|Name: John|Age: 42|Status: Active
##End Column
##End Row
##Row
##Column|75
##warning|Don't forget to check your answers
##checkAnswers|Check answers|Name:John:Change|Age:42:Change|Address:17  Mercer Gardens<br>Winchester<br>Hampshire:Change|Status:Active:Edit
##spacer|20|
##tablewheader|Table with header: Money spent per month|Date:Amount:What|Jan:£10:Shoes|Feb:£20:Trousers|Mar:£15000:Car MOT
##spacer|20|
##table|Table without header: Money spent per month|Jan:£10:Shoes|Feb:£20:Trousers|Mar:£15000:Car MOT
##spacer|20|
##tabs|Tab panel|Getting started|On the way|Arrival|Finishing off
##End Column
##End Row
##spacer|5|#ddd
##spacer|15
##h2|3. Buttons
##Row
##Column|25
##button_p|Save and continue 
##End Column
##Column|15
##button_s|Cancel
##End Column
##Column|30
##button|Example Atlas button|#fff|#00539B
##End Column
##End Row
##spacer|5|#ddd
##spacer|15
##h2|The end (nearly)
##p|And now, nearly at the end... a timeline. This is at the end of the demo to more easily show (in the editing area) how it can be written in user-friendly way, without writing all your content in one continuous block.
##row
##column|50
##timeline_ho|Creating this tool
|How it all started
:Ian
:Feb 2026
:After a quick play with CoPilot, it became clear that a tool to auto-generate html from a simple description might be feasible
|Moving forward
:Ian
:Mar 2026
:The design and code was iterated to add auto complete and lots more components
|Getting it out there
:Ian
:Apr 2026
:Successful demonstrations and use in a real design project suggest there might be lots of use cases out there...
##end column
##end row
##spinner_ho|Don't wait any longer...
##panel|Thanks for scrolling|Now go make your own content!
